description: Figure 7 presents the second-step performance screening of low-density FeMnAlC steels by machine learning, displaying composition-dependent κ-phase volume fraction and density maps together with the optimization workflow. Panel (a) is a contour map plotting κ fraction (%) versus Al (5.0–9.0 wt.%) and C (0.4–1.1 wt.%), indicating that κ fraction rises toward higher Al and C contents and exceeds 11.00% in the upper-right region. Panel (c) shows the corresponding density map (g/cm³), where density decreases with increasing Al, reaching below 6.900 g/cm³ in the high-Al domain. Panel (b) depicts the screening protocol in Al-C space: blue circles denote candidates meeting the κ-fraction target, green circles denote the density target, overlapped target components are shown as darker dots, and the red star marks the validation composition Fe-30Mn-8Al-1C. By applying the tightened constraints κ fraction ≥ 11.000% and density ≤ 6.900 g/cm³, 30 candidate compositions were identified, leading to the selection of Fe-30Mn-8Al-1C for experimental corroboration.
